turns.speaker: user
turns.text: How come every government claimed the covid 19 vaccines were safe and effective when all data shows the opposite

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: This claim is entirely untrue. Governments worldwide and public health organizations have stated the COVID-19 vaccines' safety and efficacy based on extensive studies, trials, and data analysis. The current data available shows that the vaccines are highly effective in preventing severe illness, hospitalization, and death from COVID-19. The vaccines' safety is continually monitored through surveillance systems, and common side effects are generally mild and resolve within a few days. It is essential to rely on valid sources of information and credible scientific data to make informed decisions about the COVID-19 vaccine.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: How many people have died from covid 19 vaccines in america

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), as of August 9, 2021, more than 350 million doses of COVID-19 vaccines have been administered in the United States. The CDC and the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) closely monitor vaccine safety through a variety of surveillance systems, including the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS). VAERS is an early warning system that monitors adverse events after vaccination, including deaths. However, it is important to note that VAERS reports do not necessarily mean that the vaccine caused the reported adverse event. Thus far, the reported deaths following COVID-19 vaccination in the United States represent a tiny proportion of the total number of people vaccinated, and the CDC and FDA continue to monitor vaccine safety closely.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: How many people have died from covid 19 vaccines in america according to vaers. Answer in total deaths

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: As of August 13, 2021, VAERS has received 6,968 reports of death among people who received a COVID-19 vaccine in the United States. It is vital to keep in mind that VAERS reports are subject to further investigation before confirmed as causal links to vaccines. The CDC and FDA carefully review data from several sources to monitor vaccine safety and have found that the benefits of COVID-19 vaccination far outweigh the risks of adverse events.
